title: Reader Outcomes, Publisher Stewardship, and Service Charter · Concurrent Code Workshop
summary: A public-interest newsroom incrementally builds reader-centered feed-service charter from original RSS, Atom, HTTP, and PostgreSQL fixtures while retaining prior Go, HTTP-client, HTTP-server, SQL, Git, accessibility, security, testing, cancellation, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of reader-centered feed-service charter while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal d…
objectives: Define reader group, decision, task journey, freshness need, harm, and observable value before choosing architecture.
Define respectful fetch frequency, identification, caching, opt-out, correction, deletion, and incident contacts for publishers.
Separate discovery, fetch, parse, normalize, persist, schedule, serve, and present responsibilities with explicit ports.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Feed Go Reader Decision
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Go team handles case 7647 during an isolated backup restore exercise. Build deterministic Go evidence to gate a useful reader and publisher service charter on observable decisions and inclusive evidence; failed, canceled, stale, and cross-tenant operations must preserve the prior admitted state; then inject one URL, DNS, redirect, compression, entity, depth, timeout, or publisher-budget failure and reject it safely. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Feed aggregation and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Feed Go Reader Decision.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for define reader group, decision, task journey, freshness need, harm, and observable value before choosing architecture. For Feed aggregation and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Feed Go Reader Decision, keep Feed Go Reader Decision connected to executable Go feed, XML, HTTP, PostgreSQL, schedule, API, reader, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.
